title: Unstoppable Spirit Conquers Everest
story: Xia Boyu refused to let anything stop him from reaching his dream. On May 14, 2018, this determined Chinese mountain climber achieved the incredible feat of summiting Mount Everest. What makes Xia's accomplishment even more remarkable? He's a double amputee, having lost both legs to frostbite on a previous Everest attempt. His perseverance and courage inspired climbers worldwide, proving that with willpower, even the highest peaks can be conquered.

activity_choices: Climbing the Great Wall of China

title: A Doctor's Daring Discovery
story: Dr. Edward Jenner made a bold move that changed medicine forever. In 1796, on May 14, this English country doctor tried something new to fight smallpox. Jenner's brave idea paved the way for vaccines that now save millions of lives every year.

title: Sputnik 4: The Soviet Space Pioneer
story: Zoom! A metal capsule blasted into the sky on May 14, 1960. The Soviet Union had just launched Sputnik 4, their first unmanned space capsule. This groundbreaking mission paved the way for future space exploration. Scientists packed the capsule with a dummy cosmonaut and life support systems to test how humans might survive in space. Though it never carried real astronauts, Sputnik 4's journey was a giant leap for spaceflight.

bonus_fact: Sputnik 4 carried a dummy cosmonaut to test life support systems in space.

title: Space Rock Surprise in Spain
story: Boom! A chunk of space rock crashed to Earth near Barcelona, Spain. This cosmic visitor, called the Canellas meteorite, made its grand entrance on May 14, 1861. Weighing about as much as a small dog, this 859-gram space traveler was a special type called a chondrite. Scientists were thrilled to study this rare piece of our solar system's history that fell from the sky.

title: Jazz Legend Sidney Bechet's Musical Journey
story: Sidney Bechet, a musical genius, was born in New Orleans in 1897. On May 14 of that year, the world welcomed a future jazz pioneer. Bechet mastered the clarinet and saxophone, becoming a renowned bandleader and composer. His talent shone through in songs like 'Petite Fleur', captivating audiences with soulful melodies that helped shape the jazz genre.
activity_question: What instruments did Sidney Bechet play?
bonus_fact: New Orleans, Bechet's birthplace, is often called the birthplace of jazz music.

title: Secret Agent Sonia: Born to Resist
story: Sonia d'Artois entered the world ready for adventure. Born in Eastchurch, Kent on May 14, 1924, she would grow up to become a daring British Canadian resistance agent during World War II. Sonia's bravery and quick thinking helped her outsmart enemy forces and gather crucial information. Her legacy as a real-life spy continues to inspire.
activity_question: Where was Sonia d'Artois born?
follow_up_question: What conflict was Sonia d'Artois involved in as a resistance agent?

title: A Comedy Legend's Laughter-Filled Beginning
story: Laughter echoed through Morecambe, Lancashire as Eric Morecambe took his first breath on May 14, 1926. This bouncing baby boy would grow up to become one of Britain's most beloved comedians. As half of the famous duo Morecambe & Wise, Eric's quick wit and silly antics would soon have the whole country in stitches.
activity_question: Where was Eric Morecambe born?
bonus_fact: Eric's birth name was actually John Bartholomew. He later took the name of his hometown as his stage name.
